folio.label: 109v
folio.l: And syn I ougút , a vyse me rigút wel
To whom I ©oue , my good a wey fro me
Wel meche more I ougút , a vysen me ¹de
To whom I ©eue my body , for alwey
I warne ©ow wele , it is no childes pley
To take a wyf , witú outen a vysement
Men must enquere , this is myn assent
Wheiæä she be wys , and sobre , or dronkelewe
Or proud , or ellis , other weyes a shrewe
A chidere , or a wastour of thy good
Or riche or poore , or ellis mannyssú wood
Al be it so , that no man fynden shal
Noon in this world , that trottetú hool in al
Ne man ne best , swicú as men coude deuyse
But natheles , it ougút I now suffise
Witú any wyf , if so were that she had
Moo goode thewes , than hire vices bad
And al this askitú leyser , for to enquere
For god it woot , I haue wept many a tere
Ful preuyly , syn that I hadde a wyf
Preyse who so wyl , a wedded ma~nys lyf
Certeyn I fynde in it , but cost and care
And obseruaunces , of alle blisses bare
And ©et god wot , myne neygúebores a boute
And namely of wo~men , many a route
Seyn , that I haue , the most stedefast wyf
And eke the mekest , on that beritú lyf
But I wot best , where wryngetú me my sho
ñe mounö for me , rigút as ©ow liketú do

note.notetxt: 310there is a cross in the left margin.ODR

folio.l: Auysetú ©ow , ©e ben a man of age
How that ©e entren , in to mariage
And namely , witú a ©ong wyf and a fair
Be hi~ that made , water , Erthe , and Eyr
The ©ongest man that is , in al this route
Is besy I now , to bryngen it a boute
To han his wyf a lone , trostetú me
ñe shuln nat plese hire , fully ©eres thre
This is to seyn , to don hire ful plesaunce
A wyf asketú , ful many an obseruaunce
I pray ©ow , that ©e be nat , euele a paid
¢ Wel q¶ this Ianuarie , and hast æè seid
Straw for thy xxxxx Senek , and for thyne ¸uäbes
I counte it nat xxxxx a panyere ful of herbes
Of scole tämes xxxxx wysere men than thow
As æè hast xxxxx herd , assentyd rigút now
